source_chinese: 国务院常务会议研究部署进一步稳定房地产市场的政策措施。
source_outlet: State Council executive meeting communique (deliberating real-estate stabilization measures)
agent_translation: Will China announce new real estate market stabilization measures before September 1, 2026?

ground_truth_translation.primary: Will China announce new property-market stabilization measures by August 31, 2026?
alternative_phrasings: Will the Chinese government announce additional real estate stabilization measures by August 31, 2026?
Will China's State Council or PBOC announce new property-market support measures by August 31, 2026?
Will China unveil new real-estate stabilization policies before September 1, 2026?
ground_truth_translation.rationale: Highest-ambiguity sample. Source says 'State Council researched and deployed further measures' — this is intent, not action. The question must hinge on a concrete future announcement that goes beyond the May 2026 deliberation. Two critical disambiguators: (a) what counts as 'new' (must be distinct from May 25 deliberation), (b) what counts as 'stabilization measure' (the agent translation lists examples in description — keep those; D5 requires the examples in the description). Agent title uses 'before September 1' which is functionally equivalent to 'by August 31' but the latter is the Polymarket canonical form.

polymarket_shape_validation.resolution_criteria: Official announcement by State Council, PBOC, MoHURD, or another authoritative Chinese government body of concrete new measures explicitly targeting real-estate market stabilization (e.g., lowering down-payment ratios, reducing mortgage rates, easing purchase restrictions, restructured housing-fund support). The announcement must be (1) made on or before 2026-08-31T23:59:59-04:00 and (2) distinct from / beyond the May 25, 2026 State Council deliberation.
polymarket_shape_validation.uma_dispute_risk: high - 'new' vs 'previously announced' is the dispute axis; UMA will need a clear baseline
polymarket_shape_validation.duplicate_risk: low - no real-estate-China policy markets on Polymarket in current snapshot

comparable_polymarket_markets.market: Trump eliminates capital gains tax on crypto in 2025?
Will any country leave NATO by June 30, 2026?
comparable_polymarket_markets.similarity: Will X by date pattern, government action

k5_framing_variants: Will China announce new property-market stabilization measures by August 31, 2026?
Will the State Council or PBOC announce new real-estate support policies by August 31, 2026?
Will China unveil concrete new property-market stabilization measures before September 1, 2026?
Will China's government announce additional measures (beyond the May 2026 deliberation) to stabilize the real-estate market by August 31, 2026?
Will China publish new policies aimed at stabilizing the property market by August 31, 2026?

mqm_critical_errors.detail: Agent title's 'new ... measures' does not specify 'distinct from the May 2026 deliberation that triggered this question.' If on May 26 the State Council issues a follow-up statement re-stating the same intent, it is ambiguous whether the market resolves YES. Description partially addresses this ('beyond previously announced policies') but title leaves it open.

mqm_minor_errors: STYLE/Date format: 'before September 1, 2026' is semantically equal to 'by August 31, 2026' but Polymarket convention prefers the latter compact form.

d2_stylistic.note: Length (~15 words) is in corpus median band

d4_granularity.note: Single 'announcement' event — atomic; the OR of multiple policy types in the description is acceptable disjunction

d5_resolution_clarity.note: FAILS — 'new' is under-specified at the title level; UMA dispute risk is high

d6_source_reliability.note: pbc.gov.cn is acceptable but a State Council source (gov.cn) or MoHURD (mohurd.gov.cn) would be more directly applicable

d8_duplicate_detection.note: No similar markets in corpus snapshot

annotator_notes: Agent translation is fluent but D5-failing due to under-specified 'new'. The ground truth could match agent closely on BLEU because the core phrasing is similar; main divergence is 'before September 1, 2026' vs 'by August 31, 2026' and the resolution source field. Pipeline did NOT catch that 'pbc.gov.cn' is a weak choice of resolution source for a State Council real-estate question — a real-world miss.
